meta.source: weights: datamined solarium slot23 table; rarity/cost wiki; layout inferred
meta.effect_text: While in the house, options 2 & 3 use much flatter rarity odds (datamined table).
While drafting in the Classroom: free redraws equal to your drafting-room count. 3% priority draw.
Tomorrow room: next day's starting keys +1 per Tomorrow room (cross-day; out of single-day scope).
Entering it after drafting a Drafting Room grants 10 steps (modeled unconditionally; condition Tier 2).
On enter, 3 doors unlock and the 4th locks (lock system out of scope).
Slot machine: spins cost gold (gamble not modeled; grants 1 die as approximation).
While drawn as a drafting option, a rotate action becomes available that spins every drawn floorplan to its next legal orientation (engine: Game.rotation_available/rotate_options).
Digging in rooms with locked doors unlocks them (lock system out of scope).
Each Dead End drafted in the house spreads 5 gold into the Tomb (modeled: +5 coins per dead end, granted immediately).
Always contains 2 special items (tool system out of scope; modeled as 2 random items).
Rooms more likely to contain dig spots today (dig spots out of scope; modeled as +3 luck).
Negates the effects of the next 3 Red Rooms.
Lets you pay rooms' gem costs with steps at a ratio of 3 steps to 1 gem.
Adds 8 Classrooms to today's draft pool.
Donate money for multi-day blessings (out of single-day scope).
Counts as a Shop; trade items for items (trading out of scope).
Sells permanent profile upgrades for coins (menu out of scope).

meta.source: wiki (corner-only; re-rolls the rarity of 3 pool rooms per draft)
meta.effect_text: Corner-only room; re-rolls the rarity of 3 rooms in your pool each time you draft it. Implemented as a one-time effect when the Conservatory itself is drafted: 3 random undealt deck cards each roll a fresh rarity (uniform over the 4 rarities; the real re-roll distribution is unpublished - inferred) via the dedicated conservatory_reroll RNG substream, and changed cards move to the new rarity's deck of the same free/gem class at a random undealt position (reinsertion point also inferred).

Contains one doorway per Mechanical room in the estate (including itself); extra doors beyond 4 are diagonal compartments with key/item caches. APPROXIMATION: dynamic door-count and diagonal-compartment mechanics are out of scope; no effect modeled; cross layout is a placeholder. interior_only covers the 'center 21 tiles' constraint.
